data.title: Fox reporter: US states will handle Bitcoin strategic reserves before the federal government
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that Wyoming Senator Cynthia Lummis said that "before seeing the federal Bitcoin strategic reserve, it is likely that we will see a state-level BTC strategic reserve." "Currently, there are not enough candidates in the House and Senate to do this."

data.title: Bybit hackers only laundered 14,300 ETH in the past 24 hours
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the Bybit hacker has suspended the transfer and washing of ETH since 3 pm yesterday, so only 14,300 ETH (worth about 32.2 million US dollars) have been washed away in the past 24 hours. There are still 218,000 ETH (worth about 486.6 million US dollars) in the hacker's address.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 84,000 USDT with a 0.46% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 01, 2025, 00:52 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 84,000 USDT and is now trading at 83,934.46875 USDT, with a narrowed 0.46% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Tether Treasury Destroys 2 Billion USDT on Ethereum Network
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Whale Alert has reported that approximately three hours ago, Tether Treasury destroyed 2 billion USDT on the Ethereum network.

data.title: UK Sentences First Individual For Unregistered Crypto ATM Operations
data.description:
According to PANews, the UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) announced that London resident Osunkoya has been sentenced to four years in prison for operating unregistered cryptocurrency ATMs through his company, GidiPlus Ltd. The operations took place between December 30, 2021, and March 12, 2022, across 28 different locations, involving over £2.5 million. This marks the first sentencing in the UK for unregistered crypto asset activities.

Previously, the FCA collaborated with law enforcement agencies to combat illegal crypto ATM operations nationwide. In 2023, the FCA visited 38 locations and dismantled 30 machines. The number of crypto ATMs advertised on CoinATMRadar in the UK has decreased from over 80 in 2022 to zero in 2024.

data.title: Ancient Whale Sells 14,000 ETH, Price Tests Key Support
data.description:
An early Ethereum (ETH) investor has reportedly sold a major portion of their holdings through over-the-counter (OTC) trading desk Wintermute. According to crypto analyst Ai, the whale offloaded 14,000 ETH worth approximately $31.92 million in a move that could signal growing selling pressure from long-term holders. On-chain analysis reveals the wallet’s ETH can be traced […]

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 100 Million USDC on Ethereum Network
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert has reported that approximately three hours ago, the USDC Treasury minted 100 million USDC on the Ethereum network. This development highlights ongoing activities within the cryptocurrency space as digital assets continue to gain traction.

data.title: Bloomberg: Judge dismisses SEC lawsuit against Hex token founder Richard Heart
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News A federal judge in Brooklyn, New York, dismissed the U.S. SEC's lawsuit against cryptocurrency founder Richard Heart. The founder was accused of illegally using millions of dollars of investor funds to purchase a 555-carat black diamond. The SEC accused Heart of raising more than $1 billion through the sale of unregistered securities, including Hex tokens and other digital assets related to its PulseChain blockchain network and PulseX decentralized financial platform.
The agency stated in the initial lawsuit that Heart and PulseChain used at least $12.1 million in investor funds for personal luxury purchases, including diamonds, expensive watches and high-end cars. Judge Carol Amon believed that the SEC failed to provide sufficient facts to prove that these actions occurred in the United States, allowing the SEC to amend its complaint to "correct the defects found."

data.title: 2 billion USDT destroyed by Tether Treasury
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Whale Alert monitoring, about 2 hours ago, 2 billion USDT were destroyed by Tether Treasury.

data.title: Upbit operator sues South Korea’s Financial Intelligence Unit over sanctions
data.description:
Upbit’s parent company, Dunamu, is suing South Korea’s Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) over new sanctions.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/upbit-operator-sues-fiu-unit-over-sanctions/
